name: Computational Complexity Analysis
description: Complexity analysis bounds the resources an algorithm uses as input grows: basic operations are counted as a function of n, the relevant case (worst or average) is chosen, and the dominant term gives an asymptotic class; the analysis is refined until a tight bound is established and expressed in Big-O, Theta, or Omega notation.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Algorithm + input model"]
    N2["Count basic operations"]
    N3["Express as f(n)"]
    N4{"Worst or average case?"}
    N5["Worst-case bound"]
    N6["Average-case analysis"]
    N7["Find asymptotic class"]
    N8{"Tight bound?"}
    N9["Refine analysis"]
    N10["Big-O / Theta / Omega"]
    N11["Complexity class"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|worst| N5
    N4 -->|average| N6
    N5 --> N7
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|redo| N7
    N8 -->|yes| N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Introduction to the Theory of Computation (3rd ed.)
Introduction to Algorithms (4th ed.)
sources.authors: Cormen, T. H.; Leiserson, C. E.; Rivest, R. L.; Stein, C.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
